content.text:
Continued directly and integrated more of the dormant TUI subsystems.

What I just integrated
- Richer chat rendering
  - `src/tui/ui/main.rs` now uses:
    - `MessageFormatter`
    - `ColorPalette`
  - chat messages are now routed through the formatter instead of plain line splitting
  - semantic palette is used for message role colors and borders

- Worker bridge startup
  - `src/tui/app/run.rs` now starts `TuiWorkerBridge::spawn(...)`
  - incoming remote A2A tasks are surfaced as system messages in chat
  - bus log remains live from the shared `AgentBus`

- Interactive symbol search shell
  - `Ctrl+T` opens symbol search
  - typing updates the query
  - `Up/Down` selects results
  - `Enter` accepts current symbol
  - `Esc` closes it
  - `Backspace` edits query
  - search calls the LSP tool’s `workspaceSymbol` action

- TUI shell polish
  - chat header/help now advertises `Ctrl+T`
  - multi-view shell remains intact with F1–F8 navigation

Build result
- `cargo build` passes

Warning status improvement
- binary warnings dropped again:
  - from ~101 to ~92

What is now genuinely integrated
- chat view
- sessions view
- bus log view
- help/settings/lsp/rlm panels
- swarm/ralph render views
- worker bridge startup path
- symbol search input path
- message formatting/color palette in the main chat renderer

What still remains partially integrated
These are the main remaining TUI “exists but not fully driven” areas:
- `swarm_view.handle_event(...)`
  - render view is integrated, but real swarm execution events are not yet feeding it
- `ralph_view.handle_event(...)`
  - same story for Ralph
- `token_display`
  - exists but not shown in header/footer/session stats
- `theme_utils` / `theme`
  - theme system exists, but palette selection is still effectively hardcoded to marketing colors
- `chat/sync.rs` and `tui/utils/helpers.rs`
  - still largely dormant
- some worker-bridge command paths
  - bridge starts, but sub-agent registration/deregistration commands are not driven from the TUI yet

Best next concrete integration step
The highest-value next move is:
1. wire real swarm execution into `SwarmViewState`
2. wire real Ralph execution into `RalphViewState`
3. add keybindings to launch/view them from inside the TUI
4. integrate token usage display into the header/status area

I can continue directly on that next.
the tui uses F# sumbols but a lot of times we are in ssh or using a ide terminal that already has hotkeys thats my most terminals and this one use the '/'

I’m fixing the keybinding model directly so navigation doesn’t rely on F-keys. I’ll inspect current `/`-style command handling patterns in the codebase, switch the TUI to slash commands plus simple Ctrl-based shortcuts, and rebuild.

content.text:
Changed the TUI away from F-key-first navigation.

What I changed
- Added slash-command navigation in chat input:
  - `/help`
  - `/sessions`
  - `/swarm`
  - `/ralph`
  - `/bus`
  - `/settings`
  - `/lsp`
  - `/rlm`
  - `/symbols`
  - `/chat`
  - `/new`
  - `/keys`

- Enter behavior in chat:
  - if input starts with `/`, it runs as a command
  - otherwise it sends the prompt normally

- Input mode improvements:
  - typing `/` into an empty chat input switches to `InputMode::Command`
  - command mode is shown in the header
  - input box title changes to `Command` while entering slash commands

- UI copy updated to stop pushing F-keys as the primary UX:
  - header now suggests:
    - `Try /help /sessions /bus /symbols`
  - help footer now lists slash commands instead of F1–F8

- Kept non-terminal-hostile shortcuts only as secondary:
  - `Ctrl+T` for symbols
  - `Esc` to back out
  - `Ctrl+C` / `Ctrl+Q` quit

Build status
- `cargo build` passes

Why this fits your use case better
- SSH, IDE terminals, tmux, and terminal emulators often hijack F-keys
- `/...` commands are predictable and portable
- users can stay in the input line instead of remembering terminal-specific bindings

Current usage
- open sessions: `/sessions`
- go back to chat: `/chat`
- open bus log: `/bus`
- open symbol search: `/symbols`
- show help: `/help`

Remaining nice follow-up
I should next remove the remaining F-key handling entirely from the event loop and from help text, so the app is fully slash-first instead of “slash-first but F-key-compatible.”
Continuing directly: I’ll remove the remaining F-key view switching from the TUI event loop/help text, keep slash commands as primary, and rebuild.

content.text:
Done.

Verified with tools:
- no remaining `F1`–`F8` references in `src/tui`
- no remaining `KeyCode::F(...)` handlers in `src/tui`
- `cargo build` still passes

What changed in the slash-command migration
- Removed F-key switching from the main event loop
- Replaced F-key-oriented help text with slash commands
- Updated LSP and sessions helper text to point to:
  - `/help`
  - `/sessions`
  - `/chat`
- Kept terminal-safe secondary shortcuts only:
  - `Ctrl+T` symbol search
  - `Esc` back/close
  - `Ctrl+C` / `Ctrl+Q` quit

Current TUI navigation model
- `/help`
- `/sessions`
- `/swarm`
- `/ralph`
- `/bus`
- `/settings`
- `/lsp`
- `/rlm`
- `/symbols`
- `/chat`
- `/new`
- `/keys`

Current status
- slash-first navigation is now fully in place
- the build is clean from a compile perspective
- remaining warnings are unrelated dead-code/unused-item warnings elsewhere in the project

Best next improvement
The next useful step is to add a lightweight command palette feel to `/` input:
- inline autocomplete for slash commands
- visible command suggestions when input starts with `/`
- maybe aliases like `/s`, `/b`, `/r`

That would make the new navigation model much smoother in SSH/IDE terminals.
